title: AUROVILLE | Experimental City Near Pondicherry, India

Nice and hot, but a little bit less hot than where I was previously in the interior of uh South India over at Tiruvannamalai. Yesterday I took a bus for those who have wanted to see me in a regular Indian bus instead of taking a taxi. Then I got a bus from Tiruvannamalai over here about 2 and 1/2 hours. It was 95 rupees, that is exactly $1 US. And then I got a uh room there in Pondicherry, which is about a 20-minute drive away from here for 3 nights, and then I fly out of Tamil Nadu, but a uh flight to elsewhere in India, you will find out where I'm going next later. So uh I uh was busy this morning getting my taxes done, but I got them done, and that is a massive massive relief to have that one off my mind. And then I just uh rented a motorbike to head up to Auroville. So, Auroville is an experimental town that was founded in the 1960s based on the spiritual teachings of Sri Aurobindo, and it is out there. I actually passed the uh junction to turn to go to Auroville. No signs that I saw, so uh I just went like 5 minutes further, but I wanted to show the beach anyways, and so I think this is actually the Auroville beach. Quite a nice beach. And so uh I'm getting a late start. I am trying to get to the visitor center before it's too late to get the pass to go see the Matrimandir. The Matrimandir is this huge meditation center that looks kind of like a golf ball, which they have been constructing for many decades. It is finished now, but I first visited Auroville back in 2006. So, exactly 20 years ago in January of 2006, and stayed there for a month. I was in India for 3 months on that trip. That was my second time to India. And so I also went to Bangalore, Hampi, I think Belur and Halebid. I forget where else in South India, and then to the Andaman Islands, which would have been exactly this time in uh late March 2006, 20 years ago. Now, I had wanted to go inside the Matrimandir and do the uh meditation in there, which I did in 2006, but the Matrimandir wasn't completed yet. And it was under construction outside and inside, and so I'd wanted to see it completed on the inside, but it turns out you have to get advanced reservations. You get them online. It is recommended to uh try to reserve them 3 to 7 days in advance. You can't reserve for the same day, and they are closed on Sunday and Tuesday. And so I couldn't have reserved for today because it was today already, and tomorrow they're closed, and the website only allows reservations from 7:00 in the morning until 12:00 noon, and so I had tried yesterday, but it was afternoon, and so it was all around too late. There was just nothing that I could do to be able to uh go inside the Matrimandir. So, hopefully we will get there in time to at least be able to go see it. So when I came here in 2006 and stayed for a month, I had two main objectives. One was to check out Auroville as a place to possibly move to and live here, be a uh permanent resident. And number two was to focus on writing. I had written a couple of my books. Kundalini and the Art of Being was with publishers taking forever to actually get published. And was pretty bored with uh regular life in Portland. And so that is why I decided to come here and check out uh Auroville. I enjoyed my month here, but one thing that I realized was that it was just too hot because I was here in January, the coldest month because we're in the northern hemisphere. And in January, the coldest winter month, it was flipping hot. It was even hotter than it is now, I think. But uh anyways, it was just really hot and I realized, man, if this is as cool as it gets, I guess in monsoon maybe it actually does get cooler, but then it's pouring down rain or raining a lot or whatever. And so I realized that despite being a really interesting, amazing place, I just was not into being hot and sweaty all the time. The goal is to complete Matrimandir including the park of unity and the lake as an offering to the mother for her 150th birth anniversary on 21st February 2028. So, in 2 years. So, as we enter 2026 the development work at Matrimandir is moving forward on many fronts. So, yes. Sounds like it is going to be a lake. That is going to be really beautiful when it's done. To the 12 petals surrounding the golden sphere of Matrimandir the mother gave the name of 12 qualities and assigned a specific color to each of these qualities. She also gave a name of spiritual significance to over 800 flowers. Each quality is therefore also symbolized by a flower. Generosity, goodness, courage, progress, receptivity, aspiration, perseverance, gratitude, humility, sincerity, peace, equality. Mahakali, Mahalaxmi, Maheshwari, Mahasaraswats. So, the mother was [snorts] another of the uh spiritual people who inspired the Auroville town. I think she might have actually been the one who came up with the initial idea, the vision of it. And so, the uh town and the general teachings and principles are based on her and Sri Aurobindo. But, it is not at all cult like in that all beliefs and spiritual paths, religions, etc. are all welcomed. And everybody isn't expected to subscribe to one particular set of teachings. So, really Auroville is actually a collection of different mini communities. If you stay here, then you can pick one particular place to stay which will have its own style of buildings, its own routine, its own separate price, its own emphasis perhaps as far as spiritual teachings or art or music or whatever.
